title: B2. USACO 2023 US Open Gold / Tree Merging
label: mx1_delegate_04
task_text:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: coding-B2-usaco-2023-us-open-gold-tree-merging
Title: B2. USACO 2023 US Open Gold / Tree Merging

Problem:
Reconstruct a valid sequence of sibling-merge operations that transforms an initial rooted tree into a final rooted tree.

Grading target:
Binary hidden-test acceptance.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>
root_result_excerpt: Invalid node-authored response: live provider request failed: OpenRouter request failed: The read operation timed out. Raw response excerpt:
final_result_excerpt: Invalid node-authored response: live provider request failed: OpenRouter request failed: The read operation timed out. Raw response excerpt:

run_level_variable_overrides.child_selected_prompt_text:
Solve the question directly.
Keep the entire JSON compact.
Keep decision_notes extremely short. Prefer 3 to 8 words.
Do not narrate your reasoning outside the response field.
In the response field, use exactly two short lines encoded with \n:
FINAL_ANSWER: <answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>
Keep the justification to 12 words or fewer.
Use plain text only inside the response field. Do not use markdown formatting, code fences, or math-style backslashes such as \equiv.
Do not add any extra text before or after the JSON object.

Return exactly one JSON object in this format:
{

assessment.summary: This is the first scored attempt in the loop history.

what_helped: The loop preserved the question and family boundary so changes stay interpretable.
The current prompt is isolated as the only variable being optimized.
what_hurt: The run still has room to improve in either correctness or efficiency.

recommendation.reason: The answers are still wrong across multiple attempts, so the next prompt should pivot hard to a different reasoning strategy.
recommendation.new_prompt_text: Decompose the problem into helper tasks that directly test small answer possibilities, rule out impossible cases, and compute the first viable case. Make each helper responsible for one concrete mathematical check.

history_observations: the loop has plateaued enough to justify a new direction
